standard_refs.title: RFC 1034 - Domain Names - Concepts and Facilities
RFC 1035 - Domain Names - Implementation and Specification
standard_refs.notes: Message format, label length, compression, and RR encoding.

standard_refs.title: RFC 9267 - DNS RR Processing Anti-Patterns
standard_refs.notes: Implementation anti-patterns tied to DNS RR processing.

standard_refs.title: RFC 9520 - Negative Caching of DNS Resolution Failures

reason_for_selection: Very rich CVE and advisory material.
Strong implicit constraints around compression, parser termination, cache integrity, and authority boundaries.
Must be scoped narrowly to avoid resolver-wide complexity.

potential_implicit_constraint_points.description: Pointers must resolve within packet bounds, avoid loops, and preserve the 255-octet expanded-name limit.
Cache insertion must preserve bailiwick/request relevance and avoid trusting forged or unsolicited records.

notes: First DNS work should be limited to parser/compression or a single cache-boundary case.
